observations.recommendation:
The Management establishes clear-cut guidelines for the implementation of AICS program in line with DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, series of 2019, and its amendments, with the concurrence of the Sangguniang Bayan to strengthen its internal control on the disbursement of public funds.

Ensures that all applications for financial assistance under the AICS program undergo assessment by the MSWDO and favorable recommendation is issued thereto before actual disbursement of funds.
The Management returns the remaining funds to the Bureau of Treasury so that the government can use it for the implementation of identified priority programs, projects and activities.
The Management restores the cash in the municipal treasury.

Municipal Accountant assesses the collectability of the subject receivables and set up an impairment and make a request for write-off, as may be warranted, in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2016-005.

The Inventory Committee of the Municipality conducts the physical count of inventory, prepare RPCI based on the result of the physical count, and submit the same to the auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS, Volume I, and to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.

The Municipal Accountant undertakes reconciliation of inventory records with the RPCI, and make necessary adjustments, where appropriate.
Submits the liquidation of all implemented projects and remit the remaining balances thereof.

Bids and Awards Committee furnishes the Audit Team with copies of perfected contracts for infrastructure projects within five (5) days from perfection thereof to enable the Audit Team to conduct auditorial/legal review of these documents at the most appropriate time and manner pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

Inspection Officer furnishes copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered to enable the latter to conduct timely validation/inspection thereof, pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.

Management submits the legal basis and specific guidelines for payment of the food allowance to facilitate the verification of the propriety of the amount claimed in accordance with Section 1.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001.

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer submit the monthly Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAAOB) on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU.

Management considers assigning a person in charge of inventory in the Rural Health Unit.

The Municipal Health Officer practices the preparation of requisition issue slip and properly monitor the issuance of drugs and medicines in accordance with Section 41 of the New Government Accounting System Volume II.

Management assigns a focal person for the monitoring and submission of the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels and ensure that the required reports like the Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished.
Municipal Agriculturist regularly monitors the changes of Breeding Stocks to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account.

We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Inform the Local Sanggunian to revisit and re-evaluate their ordinance and are willing to amend the ordinance of the health program since there is a necessity to amend the procedural guidelines so that charges thereof will be in accordance with the accounting and auditing rules. b. We also recommended that the Local Sanggunian evaluate if there is a need to augment or increase the budget for the health program so that all hospital charges during the year will be paid and recorded in the year incurred, to align with the provisions of the Local Government Code. c. Further, we recommended that the City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts specifically in the Trust Fund relating to the Baskug Nga Barangay Fund using the following adjusting entries: Donation ₱15,688,578.78 Subsidy from Other Funds ₱15,688,578.78 To record adjustments for the charges for year 2024 d. Finally, we recommended that management stop the transfer of the funds for Baskug nga Barangay Program from the General Fund to the Trust Fund to avoid confusion in the recognition and charging of expenses. Instead, recognize all current year charges in the General Fund to arrive at reliable account balances.

We recommended that the City Accountant coordinate with the City Treasurer when recording checks disbursements. For checks already released, the DV and supporting documents together with the RCI shall be forwarded to the Accounting Office for proper recording; if not yet released, the related liability account shall be set-up instead of deducting the amount outright from the Cash-In-Bank account. Further, we recommended that, henceforth, the process in the disbursements of checks in accordance with Section 43 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs be followed.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from placing the SEF and TF in high yield time deposits since these are not idle funds as defined in the aforementioned COA guidelines. Apparently, all existing TD placement of the SEF and TF shall be reverted to the regular current account once they reach their maturity dates.

We recommended that Management: a. Require the event focal person to notify the Local Barangay and directly charge it to the general fund. The Sanggunian shall then reevaluate their ordinance and are willing to amend the ordinance of the health program since there is a necessity to amend the procedural guidelines so that charges thereof will be in accordance with the accounting and auditing rules. b. In case the sellers will not coordinate or refuse to pay the SP, it shall be the joint liabilities of the concerned local officials and employees to pay the amount collectible to the city government. c. Henceforth, ensure that in similar events, the focal persons and the collecting office communicate and update any concerns to the City Treasurer to prevent recurrence of non-payment.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit to the Audit Team the projects’ technical supporting documents as enumerated in Section 3.1.3 of COA Circular 2009-01 to facilitate the conduct of the contract and technical evaluation.

We recommended that Management require the City Engineering/General Services Offices to prepare the POW of projects in accordance with DPWH Department Order No. 197 to ensure an accurate project cost estimate. Further, we recommended that management require the contractor of the abovementioned project to refund the excess amount of ₱14,115.34 which was included as costs of construction health and safety works.

We recommend that Management require the City Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments using the suggested adjusting journal entries: Trust Liability- Sale from bid docs ₱ 559,707.53 Cash in Bank ₱559,707.53 To record transfer of balance of the proceeds from sale of bidding documents to the general fund. Going forward, the management will ensure timely transfers of unutilized fund balances from the proceeds of sale of bidding documents. Any excess amount collected will be reverted to the General Fund, in accordance with Section 6.2 of DBM Circular No. 2004-5A.
We recommended and Management agreed that disbursement of funds has to be charged to the General Fund in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the City HRMO review the personnel complement of the city government and promote the regularization of the eligible job order personnel so that regular functions are assigned to them as prescribed in the laws, rules and regulations of human resource management. In addition, it is also recommended that the City Budget Officer and City HRMO review the existing vacant positions vis-à-vis the allowable PS limitation to determine the vital new positions that will be created to augment and mobilize the workforce for effective and efficient delivery of basic services.

We recommended that: a. The Internal Audit Services perform a personnel audit in order to assess the performance, functions and necessity as support group to deliver the basic services for the constituents. b. The HRMO hire only the necessary manpower to augment the barangay workforce to ensure faster delivery of basic services limiting their job description to perform non-crucial tasks in the barangay. c. The HRMO see to it that official logbooks have to be properly maintained for JOs assigned in the field. Further, we recommend that management establish a mechanism to monitor the whereabouts of the JOs especially those that are assigned in the field. d. The time keepers to monitor and perform their functions efficiently so that all JOs record their attendance daily.

We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions by hiring new employees or expedite promotion of deserving employees to enhance its services to attain full implementation of PPAs of the city government for the optimal benefit of the constituents as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community.

We recommended that management require the different departments to enhance the preparation of the LBP Form No. 4 for the preparation of the ensuing year Annual Budget so that it will present a comprehensive budget which is one of the goals in the Budget Operations Manual. We also remind that said form has to be duly signed by the process owners and accountable officials as required.

We recommended that Management require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and the Budget Officer to review the amount appropriated for the LDRRMF so that it will be sourced out solely from the regular sources, excluding the revenues from economic enterprises in accordance with the guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed to fully utilize the 20 per cent Development Fund pursuant to the intents of DBM-DILG-DOF JMC No.1 by ensuring that only PPAs which are ready for implementation are included in the plan and that procurement timelines are followed to avoid delays in project implementation.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to submit GAD Accomplishment Report to the Audit Team within five working days from the end of January to facilitate timely monitoring and evaluation and determine whether the activities based on the DILG approved GAD Plan and Budget were implemented.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to prioritize completion of the gathering of the data/information needed for the creation of a well-established GAD database to have an accurate and reliable basis in the planning and programming of PPAs as well as in the policy formulation, in order to properly address the existing and emerging GAD issues and concerns.

We recommended to Management that: a. Separate departments shall be assigned for the preparation of the general payrolls and the remittances of mandatory deductions and government shares. b. The Accounting Department check the accuracy of the remittances based on the amount deducted in the employees’ respective payrolls. c. The Accounting Department set up receivables from employees with overpaid contribution, if any.

Management exhausts all possible remedies provided under the laws, rules, and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance with the audit suspensions and the refund/settlement of the disallowances and charges as provided in the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize withholding tax upon recording the liability at year-end by crediting Due to BIR and recording Accounts Payable at net of tax to minimize the risk of administrative penalties and ensure the accurate presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare a journal entry to restore any unreleased checks to the Cash in Bank account. We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to include in the RCI only the checks released to claimants which shall be the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the preparation of JEV and recording in the Check Disbursements Journal.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct all concerned personnel, including drivers, heads of office, and the inspectorate team, to provide written explanations regarding the above observations detailing their involvement in the procurement, issuance, and consumption of fuel related to the aforementioned motor vehicles for the audit team’s evaluation.

We recommended and management agreed to revisit HDMF’s Circular No. 460 and submit justification on the excessive personal and government share for the employees’ Pag-IBIG contribution. Further, we recommended and management agreed to apprise the employees of the required amount of share for the contribution and inquire whether they elect to have the shares deducted from their salary to be more than the required amount.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to adopt measures to strengthen the controls in the issuance and remittance of cash ticket collections by requiring its prompt and full remittance by the sub-collectors to prevent exposure of government funds to the risk of loss, theft or misappropriation.

We recommended and management agreed to require the collectors to remit their collections intact daily to the liquidating officer in order to safeguard the government funds from loss, theft or misappropriation.

We recommended and management agreed to close the dormant DBP and FCB accounts and transfer the remaining balances to the LGU’s active accounts to better support its operations and ensure the efficient use of funds.

We recommended and management agreed to require the contractors of the aforementioned projects to post a warranty security and, if the defects liability period has lapsed with no claims filed, issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to obtain the cash advance for the confidential fund at the beginning of the covered quarter to ensure that the funds are used exclusively for their intended period and to prevent the reimbursement of prior expenses, in accordance with applicable regulations.

We recommended and management agreed to send demand letters to the concerned municipal officers and employees to enforce immediate settlement of the outstanding cash advances and make a proper accounting or liquidation thereof.

We recommended and management agreed to require the requisitioning officer to issue a certification, in support of the purchase requests, that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition pursuant to EO No. 49.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to compute and withhold CWT from the monthly allowance provided to the DTTB pursuant to Section 2 of the RR 11-2018.

We recommended and management agreed to discontinue paying internet bills through reimbursement and instead issue checks directly to the service provider, ensuring that the withholding tax is properly computed and deducted in accordance with RR 14-2002.

We recommended and management agreed to require the LDRRMC to revise the LDRRMFIP following the COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, by incorporating the detailed plan for the prior years’ unexpended balances recorded in the Trust Liabilities – LDRRM. Further, the management agreed to ensure that disbursements shall only be made out of the unexpended balance if included in the revised LDRRMFIP approved by the Sangguniang Bayan.
We recommended and management agreed that the MAO ensure that the tractor and backhoe are stored in their designated area as intended.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that, for infrastructure projects, a tarpaulin signboard is displayed at the project location, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 to disseminate information to the public regarding the implementation of government projects, thereby promoting transparency, accountability, and public awareness.

We recommended and management agreed to require the concerned employee receiving cash advances for the registration of motor vehicles to apply for fidelity bond with the Bureau of the Treasury in order to ensure proper coverage and guarantee indemnification in case of loss of funds.
We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to release RPT and CTC shares to the barangays within five (5) days after the end of each quarter.

observations.reason: Of the reported balance of ₱111,457,586.56 for the NGAs account in 2021, which was ₱100,045,546.91 has already been liquidated or settled during the period from 2022 to March 2024, with a remaining balance of ₱11,289,143.30 as of October 16, 2024. An appeal for the ND has been submitted to the COA National Office; however, there is no update on the status of the appeal.

observations.recommendation: Disqualifies the concerned NGOs/POs with unliquidated fund transfers for new fund transfers until their previous fund transfers are liquidated pursuant to COA Circular No. 2007- 001 dated October 25, 2007; and Verifies the actual ages of the unliquidated fund transfers and request for authority to write-off if the ages of such fund transfers qualify them as dormant unliquidated fund transfers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;
observations.managementAction: Stricten the requisites for transfers as grant of financial assistance. Require NGO/PO to submit liquidation report.
observations.reason: In line with the COA recommendation to disqualify NGOs/POs with unliquidated fund transfers from receiving new transfers, we have added a requirement for a Certificate of No Unliquidated Financial Assistance in the pre-audit checklist, as part of the necessary documents before granting new financial assistance. As of August 28, 2025, the unliquidated funds due from NGOs/POs amounting to ₱16,533,000.00 had been reduced to ₱10,930,000.00, which includes ₱10,000,000.00 financial assistance disallowed and pending appeal at the COA Central Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management make use of the free accounting system provided by the Commission on Audit through compliance with the requirements for e-NGAS installation for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate more reliable and accurate balances of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person facilitates the gathering of data/information needed for the creation of a well-established GAD database to have an accurate and reliable basis in the planning and programming of PPAs as well as in the policy formulation, in order to properly address the existing and emerging GAD issues and concerns.
observations.reason: GAD database is already established and will be further enriched with the CBMS Data.

observations.recommendation: The City Accountant, City Treasure and City Assessor ensure that the set-up of RPT and SET Receivables and their corresponding deferred income accounts is in compliance with Section 20, Chapter 3 Volume 1 of the MNGAS.
observations.managementAction: RPT and SET receivables shall be set up at the beginning of the year based on the Assessment Roll
observations.reason: The RPT and SET receivables set up at the beginning of the year were based on the Assessment Roll from the City Assessor’s Office, not on BLGF Targets.

observations.recommendation: Management, thru the City Assessor’s Office and the Property Unit of the General Services Office, exert further efforts to locate the documents establishing the legality of ownership of the subject lots thru proper coordination with the heads of concerned departments, and exhaust all possible means to register/transfer ownership under the Torrens Title System. • File Application to obtain copies of the OCT and TCT from the Register of Deeds.
observations.managementAction: CGSO and Casso shall undertake the following: • Tracing of lot history of the parcels of land; • Gathering and consolidating of all requirements for the processing and filing of Foreshore Lease Application and Special Patent at DENR; • File Application to obtain copies of the OCT and TCT from the Register of Deeds.
observations.reason: Of the 38 parcel of land that are not registered under the Torrens Title System, 6 are currently being processed for Foreshore Lease Agreement, 4 have pending application for Special Land Use Permit, 2 gave Special Patent Application, 6 recovered titles, 6 school sites, 8 were reconciled parcel of lot which has been re-issued with new title number, 1 has already acquired a new title. CGSO continues to track the remaining parcels of land.

observations.recommendation: The City Treasurer provides the final computation of RPT/SET, including penalties on unpaid real property, and demands immediate payment of the same in order that the government may not be deprived of additional revenues to finance other developmental projects.
observations.managementAction: Collect the amounts and send demand letters. Regular sending of demand letters.
observations.reason: Majority of the amounts are already collected and demand letters were also sent to concerned taxpayers. Some of the Taxpayers claimed tax exemption based on the City’s Incentive Code. Further, Management initiates a demand from Belian Inns and Hotel Inc. for payment of their business taxes.

observations.recommendation: The Management makes use of the free accounting system provided by the Commission on Audit through compliance with the requirements for e-NGAS installation for an efficient and effective computerized accounting system that will generate more reliable and accurate balances of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Continue the data build-up process in preparation for migrating the accounting data from the current accounting system to e-NGAS. Management shall likewise benchmark this year the usage of e-NGAS and will conduct a benchmarking activity with an existing e-NGAS user, to gain insights from their experience, identify best practices and challenges, and determine requirements for our own implementation.
observations.reason: Currently conducting a data build up before fully migrating accounting data to e-NGAS.

observations.recommendation: The City Accountant exerts extra effort to determine the composition of the Payable accounts totaling ₱4,648,165.78 in order to ascertain its validity and to effect remittance or payments thereof.
observations.managementAction: Initiate a thorough reconciliation of inter-agency payables, including both current and prior years’ balances. And assign a personnel to undertake this task to determine the validity and accuracy of inter-agency payable accounts.
observations.reason: In response to the COA’s recommendation, we have initiated a thorough reconciliation of inter-agency payables, including both current and prior years’ balances. Currently, a personnel is assigned to undertake this task to determine the validity and accuracy of inter-agency payable accounts. Upon completion of the reconciliation, we will effect necessary remittance or payments for valid payables and take appropriated actions for any discrepancies or invalid accounts.

Henceforth, limit the grant and payment of the monetization of leave credits only to employees who have accumulated substantial vacation and sick leave credit balances in order to qualify for availment of the monetary benefit.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management create additional permanent position and hire personnel to assist the Municipal Accountant in order to effectively discharge the functions of the accounting office especially in the preparation and submission of the monthly and quarterly trial balances and other required reports.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management submit the Disbursement Vouchers and all supporting documents of the Cash Advance for Confidential Fund to the COA ATL and henceforth comply with the requirements set forth under Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of the COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and the DND specifically on the submission of cash advance disbursement and supporting documents within seven (7) days after release of the check.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management properly communicate the release of wages to casuals and job orders to prevent unclaimed wages in the hands of the DO. Furthermore, the DO is recommended to liquidate her cash advances in accordance with COA Circular 97-002 to prevent the delays in the recognition of expenses and preparation of reports.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the property custodian of the municipality to have all motor vehicles owned by the municipal government, except security vehicle, marked “For Official Use Only”, under which shall be written the information required under COA Circular no. 77-61. Furthermore, see to it that all government vehicles be registered and issued with the corresponding government plate.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management stop the practice of effecting payment of transactions with incomplete signatories. They have to exercise utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities and observe proper internal control in the disbursements of public funds.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the cancellation/adjustment of the stale checks in the reconciling items giving priority to long outstanding prior year’s issuances in order to reflect the correct balance of the asset account and the fair presentation of the financial statements at year end as required under Section 59 of the GAAM.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due from Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the GAD Focal Person of the Municipality immediately submit the GAD Accomplishment Report to COA. Further, we recommend that the Chairperson of GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the municipal employees and general public pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommend that management properly monitor and control its fuel requisition and consumption and comply with Section 361 (e) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) by requiring all officials and employees concerned and responsible in the preparation and approval of trip tickets as well as authorized passengers to ensure that driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip.
